title: AitelierRequestExtension
description: The `aitelier` block accepted on /v1/chat/completions (and /v1/runs) when `model` starts with `agent:`. Carries agent-execution knobs that don't fit OpenAI's request shape.

workspace.description: Path inside the sandbox the agent should operate on. Maps to ACP `session/new.cwd`.

mcp_servers.description: MCP servers exposed to the agent. Maps to ACP `session/new.mcpServers`. Each entry is an MCP server descriptor (name, transport, url/command).

tool_allowlist.description: Tools the agent is allowed to invoke. Claude-only: passed via `session/new` `_meta.claudeCode.options.allowedTools`. Rejected (400) on other backends, which have no ACP channel for it — use `approval_mode` for tool access there.

max_turns.description: Cap on agent reasoning turns. Claude-only: passed via `session/new` `_meta.claudeCode.options.maxTurns`. Rejected (400) on other backends; bound those runs with the top-level `timeout` instead.

reasoning_effort.description: Inner-agent reasoning effort, mapped to the backend's advertised `thought_level` option (codex: low/medium/high/xhigh; claude: low/medium/high/xhigh/max). Validated against the backend's advertised values; an unknown value fails fast. Falls back to the top-level OpenAI `reasoning_effort` when unset.

approval_mode.description: Inner-agent approval / sandboxing preset, mapped to the backend's advertised `mode` option (codex: read-only/auto/full-access; claude: auto/default/acceptEdits/plan/dontAsk/bypassPermissions). Validated against advertised values.

prepare.description: Pre-flight sandbox setup. Shape: {install_agents, commands, files, sidecars}. Each phase runs in order; a failure aborts before the agent starts.

install_agents.description: Agent backends to JIT-install before the run.

sidecars.description: Long-lived processes started before the agent runs and torn down after.

artifacts.description: Post-flight file fetch from the sandbox.

fetch.description: Paths to pull back after the agent finishes. Returned under `aitelier_artifacts` keyed by path.

trace_tag.description: Free-form tag stamped on the run row. Query via `/v1/runs?trace_tag=...`.

parent_run_id.description: Optional pointer to a parent run for multi-agent workflows. Pure pass-through: recorded on this child's row, queryable via `/v1/runs?parent_run_id=X`. Aitelier enforces no semantics (no FK, no cycle check, no cascade). Orchestrators above aitelier own the meaning.

examples.description: Few-shot examples. Each `{user, assistant}` pair is folded into the system prompt under `## Examples`.

allow_tool_drop.description: Escape hatch: when true, OpenAI `tools` / `tool_choice` are silently dropped on the agent path instead of 400ing. For consumers whose transport sends `tools` per global config and can't suppress per-profile (e.g. Hermes).
